private_memo:
# Morning memo — 2026-06-13

**Source failures:** None observed.

## Worth attention

- **Homebrew 6.0.0** — https://brew.sh/2026/06/11/homebrew-6.0.0/
  Homebrew 6.0.0 ships with a new tap trust security mechanism, a faster JSON API, and Linux sandboxing. If you use Homebrew, run `brew update`. The tap trust model adds real security for third-party taps.

- **Anthropic apologizes for invisible Claude Fable guardrails** — https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/948280/anthropic-claude-fable-invisible-distillation-guardrail
  Anthropic acknowledged Claude Fable shipped with an undisclosed distillation guardrail that silently degraded output. They've apologized and promised more transparency. If you build on the Claude API, be aware that hidden behavior modifiers can affect your product.

- **Hundreds of AUR packages attacked by infostealer** — https://lists.archlinux.org/archives/list/aur-general@lists.archlinux.org/thread/FGXPCB3ZVCJIV7FX323SBAX2JHYB7ZS4/
  Active supply chain attack: hundreds of Arch User Repository packages were compromised with infostealer malware. If you use AUR, check the affected package list immediately and audit recent installs.

- **AI agent bankrupted their operator while trying to scan DN42** — https://lantian.pub/en/article/fun/ai-agent-bankrupted-their-operator-scan-dn42lantian.lantian/
  An autonomous agent ran up massive costs scanning DN42 without spending controls. If you run autonomous agents with network or API access, implement hard spending limits and kill switches.

- **Zed introduces DeltaDB** — https://zed.dev/blog/introducing-deltadb
  Zed is building DeltaDB to track file changes between commits. Could change how editors and AI assistants understand in-progress work. Worth watching if you care about dev tooling evolution.

- **Claude Fable is relentlessly proactive (Simon Willison)** — https://simonwillison.net/2026/Jun/11/fable-is-relentlessly-proactive/
  Simon Willison's hands-on report: Claude Fable 5 is "relentlessly proactive," deploying many techniques to reach goals. Useful context for tuning Claude-based agent workflows.

- **MiMo Code is now released and open-source** — https://mimo.xiaomi.com/mimocode
  Xiaomi released MiMo Code, an open-source code model. Another option for local inference via Ollama if you're evaluating alternatives to DeepSeek Coder.

- **Chat SDK adds AgentPhone support** — https://vercel.com/changelog/chat-sdk-adds-agentphone-support
  Vercel Chat SDK now supports voice calls and SMS from a single webhook via AgentPhone. Relevant if you're building voice agents — one endpoint handles both channels.

## Full digest
- [P] [hn-frontpage] Homebrew 6.0.0 — https://brew.sh/2026/06/11/homebrew-6.0.0/ — Major release: tap trust security, faster JSON API, Linux sandboxing
- [P] [hn-frontpage] Anthropic apologizes for invisible Claude Fable guardrails — https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/948280/anthropic-claude-fable-invisible-distillation-guardrail — Hidden distillation guardrail degraded output quality
- [P] [hn-frontpage] Hundreds of AUR packages attacked by infostealer — https://lists.archlinux.org/archives/list/aur-general@lists.archlinux.org/thread/FGXPCB3ZVCJIV7FX323SBAX2JHYB7ZS4/ — Supply chain attack on Arch User Repository
- [P] [hn-frontpage] AI agent bankrupted their operator scanning DN42 — https://lantian.pub/en/article/fun/ai-agent-bankrupted-their-operator-scan-dn42lantian.lantian/ — Agent without cost controls ran up massive bill

results.title: Homebrew 6.0.0
results.summary: Homebrew 6.0.0 ships with a new tap trust security mechanism, a faster and smaller default JSON API, sandboxing on Linux, and better defaults informed by telemetry. If you use Homebrew on macOS or Linux, run `brew update` to get it. The tap trust model is the most notable change — it adds a security layer for third-party taps that wasn't there before.
results.raw_claim: Homebrew 6.0.0 introduces tap trust security, a new default JSON API, and Linux sandboxing.
results.worth_mentioning_reason: Every Mac developer uses Homebrew. The tap trust model changes how you should think about third-party taps.
results.verdict_reason: Major version of a tool every Mac dev uses. Tap trust and Linux sandboxing are security-relevant.

results.title: Anthropic apologizes for invisible Claude Fable guardrails
results.summary: Anthropic acknowledged that Claude Fable shipped with an undisclosed distillation guardrail that silently degraded output quality in certain scenarios. The guardrail was invisible to users and developers. Anthropic has apologized and said it will be more transparent about such mechanisms going forward. If you're building on the Claude API, this is worth knowing — hidden behavior modifiers can affect your product without your knowledge.
results.raw_claim: Anthropic shipped Claude Fable with an invisible distillation guardrail that silently degraded output quality.
results.uncertainty: Details on which scenarios were affected and how output quality was degraded are limited.
results.worth_mentioning_reason: Directly relevant to anyone building on Claude. Hidden guardrails affecting output quality is a trust issue for API consumers.
results.verdict_reason: API trust issue for anyone building on Claude. Changes how you evaluate model behavior.

results.title: Hundreds of AUR packages attacked by infostealer
results.summary: Hundreds of Arch User Repository packages were compromised with infostealer malware. A list of affected packages has been published. If you use Arch Linux or install anything from AUR, check the affected package list immediately. This is a supply chain attack — the packages were modified to exfiltrate credentials and other sensitive data.
results.raw_claim: Hundreds of AUR packages were compromised with infostealer malware in a supply chain attack.
results.uncertainty: Scope of actual infections and data exfiltration is still being assessed.
results.worth_mentioning_reason: Active supply chain attack affecting a major Linux package repository. Immediate action required for Arch users.
results.verdict_reason: Active supply chain attack. Immediately actionable for anyone using AUR.

results.title: AI agent bankrupted their operator while trying to scan DN42
results.summary: A cautionary tale of an autonomous AI agent that ran up massive costs while attempting to scan the DN42 network. The agent kept spawning actions without cost controls and burned through the operator's budget. If you run autonomous agents — especially ones with network access or API-calling abilities — this is a concrete reminder to implement hard spending limits and kill switches.
results.raw_claim: An autonomous AI agent ran up excessive costs scanning DN42 due to lack of spending controls.
results.uncertainty: Exact cost figures and which agent framework was used are unclear from the title alone.
results.worth_mentioning_reason: Directly relevant to anyone running autonomous agents. Concrete cost-control lesson.
results.verdict_reason: Cautionary agent cost story. Directly relevant to autonomous agent builders.

results.title: Zed introduces DeltaDB: tracking changes between commits
results.summary: Zed is building DeltaDB, a system for tracking file changes between commits — capturing the work that happens in the gaps. This could change how editors understand and assist with in-progress code. Interesting for dev tooling builders watching the IDE space evolve beyond git-centric workflows.
results.raw_claim: Zed is building DeltaDB to track file changes between git commits for better editor-assisted development.
results.uncertainty: Early-stage feature; unclear when it ships or how it integrates with existing git workflows.
results.worth_mentioning_reason: Novel approach to dev tooling that could influence how editors and AI assistants understand code changes.
results.verdict_reason: Novel dev tooling concept from Zed. Worth tracking for future IDE/agent integration ideas.

results.title: Claude Fable is relentlessly proactive (Simon Willison)
results.summary: Simon Willison's hands-on assessment of Claude Fable 5 after two days: the model is 'relentlessly proactive,' deploying many techniques to reach its goal. He provides concrete examples of the model's behavior patterns. Useful context for anyone tuning Claude-based agent workflows to understand what Fable optimizes for.
results.raw_claim: Simon Willison reports that Claude Fable 5 is relentlessly proactive in deploying techniques to achieve goals.
results.worth_mentioning_reason: Builder report from a credible source on Claude Fable 5 behavior, relevant to agent workflow design.
results.verdict_reason: Credible builder report on Claude Fable behavior. Useful for agent workflow design.

results.title: MiMo Code is now released and open-source
results.summary: Xiaomi released MiMo Code, an open-source code model. For solo devs running local models via Ollama, this is another option to evaluate for code assistance. Worth benchmarking against existing options like DeepSeek Coder and Code Llama if you're already running local inference.
results.raw_claim: Xiaomi released MiMo Code as an open-source code model.
results.uncertainty: No independent benchmarks yet. Unclear how it compares to established open-source code models.
results.worth_mentioning_reason: New open-source code model option for local inference users.
results.verdict_reason: New open-source code model. Relevant to local Ollama users.

results.title: Chat SDK adds AgentPhone support
results.summary: Vercel's Chat SDK now supports AgentPhone, letting bots handle voice calls and SMS from a single webhook. Calls and messages to the same number land in one thread, maintaining context. Relevant for voice agent builders — one endpoint handles both channels, reducing integration complexity.
results.raw_claim: Vercel Chat SDK added AgentPhone adapter for voice calls and SMS from a single webhook endpoint.
results.uncertainty: Pricing and production reliability of AgentPhone are unclear.
results.worth_mentioning_reason: Simplifies voice agent integration to a single webhook. Relevant to CalenCall-type projects.
results.verdict_reason: Voice+SMS via single webhook. Relevant to CalenCall and voice agent work.

results.title: The RCE that AMD wouldn't fix
results.summary: Follow-up on an AMD remote code execution vulnerability that AMD has declined to patch. Previously reported in February 2026.
results.raw_claim: AMD continues to decline patching a known remote code execution vulnerability in its hardware.
results.uncertainty: Which specific AMD products are affected and practical exploitability remain debated.
results.worth_mentioning_reason: Ongoing unpatched hardware RCE is worth tracking.
results.verdict_reason: Ongoing unpatched RCE, but previously reported and no new practical impact.

results.title: macOS 27 Beta breaks the ability to boot Asahi Linux
results.summary: The macOS 27 beta breaks Asahi Linux booting on Apple Silicon. If you dual-boot, don't install the beta.
results.raw_claim: macOS 27 beta breaks Asahi Linux boot on Apple Silicon Macs.
results.uncertainty: Beta software — may be fixed before release.
results.worth_mentioning_reason: Relevant for Apple Silicon users who dual-boot Linux.
results.verdict_reason: Beta breakage — track but don't act until release.

results.title: datasette 1.0a33
results.summary: Datasette 1.0a33 extends the ?_extra= pattern to queries and rows, a significant step toward stable 1.0.
results.raw_claim: Datasette 1.0a33 extends the _extra pattern to queries and rows as a step toward stable 1.0 release.
results.worth_mentioning_reason: Datasette approaching 1.0 is worth tracking for data exploration workflows.
results.verdict_reason: Alpha release of a useful data tool. Track for 1.0.

results.title: Build custom Slack runtimes
results.summary: Vercel Chat SDK ships Slack primitives as standalone imports.
results.raw_claim: Vercel Chat SDK now offers standalone Slack adapter primitives.
results.verdict_reason: Niche Chat SDK feature, low relevance to solo dev workflow.

results.title: Chat SDK adds Velt support
results.summary: Vercel Chat SDK adds Velt adapter for comment thread bots.
results.raw_claim: Vercel Chat SDK added Velt comment thread adapter.

results.title: Introducing Vercel Drop
results.summary: Drag-and-drop deploy to Vercel without Git or CLI.
